evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to this Flask app. The service is at examples/python/flask-basic/
evals.expected_output: OTel SDK installed, Flask auto-instrumentation configured, OTLP exporter wired

dependencies: opentelemetry-api is added to pyproject.toml or requirements.txt
opentelemetry-sdk is added to pyproject.toml or requirements.txt
sdk_init: A separate OTel initialization file is created (not inline in app.py)
TracerProvider is configured with a Resource containing service.name
OTLPSpanExporter or OTLPTraceExporter is configured
SDK is initialized exactly once per process
TextMapPropagator is set (TraceContextTextMapPropagator or composite)
auto_instrumentation: FlaskInstrumentor().instrument_app(app) is called or equivalent auto-instrumentation is wired
spans: HTTP server spans are produced by auto-instrumentation for all routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/<id>, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/<id>, DELETE /tasks/<id>
Span names follow OTel HTTP semconv: low-cardinality, e.g. GET /tasks/{task_id} not GET /tasks/42
http.request.method, http.route, http.response.status_code attributes are present on HTTP spans
Span status is set to ERROR on 5xx responses
metrics: http.server.request.duration histogram is produced by auto-instrumentation
config: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME is configurable via environment variable
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT defaults to http://localhost:4318 or is env-driven
No hardcoded endpoints or tokens in source code
error_handling: Span status is set to ERROR on unhandled exceptions or 5xx responses
recordException is called on caught exceptions

evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry to this Go chi service. The code is at examples/go/chi-basic/
evals.expected_output: OTel SDK initialized, otelhttp or otelchi middleware wired, OTLP exporter configured

sdk_init: A separate OTel initialization file is created (e.g. otel.go), not inline in main()
TracerProvider is created with a Resource containing service.name
OTLPTraceExporter is configured
TracerProvider is registered globally via otel.SetTracerProvider()
TextMapPropagator is set via otel.SetTextMapPropagator()
Graceful shutdown is wired via defer or signal handling
auto_instrumentation: otelhttp.NewHandler wraps the chi router as the outermost handler for spans and metrics
spans: HTTP server spans are produced for all routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/{id}, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/{id}, DELETE /tasks/{id}
Span names are low-cardinality (route templates, not resolved paths)
http.request.method, http.route, http.response.status_code attributes are present
Span status is set to ERROR on 5xx responses
metrics: http.server.request.duration histogram is produced by otelhttp middleware
config: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME is read from environment or set via resource attribute
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT defaults to http://localhost:4318 or is env-driven
No hardcoded endpoints or tokens in source code
error_handling: Span status is set to ERROR on failures or panics
span.RecordError is called on caught errors or recovered panics

evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to this Express app. The service is at examples/node/express-basic/
evals.expected_output: OTel NodeSDK configured, HTTP+Express auto-instrumentation enabled, OTLP exporter wired

sdk_init: A separate instrumentation file is created (e.g. instrumentation.js or tracing.js)
NodeSDK or TracerProvider is configured with a Resource containing service.name
OTLPTraceExporter is configured
SDK is initialized exactly once, loaded before the app via --require or --import
Graceful shutdown is wired via process.on('SIGTERM') or similar
auto_instrumentation: HttpInstrumentation is registered in the SDK instrumentations array
ExpressInstrumentation is registered in the SDK instrumentations array
spans: HTTP server spans are produced for all routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/:id, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/:id, DELETE /tasks/:id
Span names follow OTel HTTP semconv: low-cardinality route templates
http.request.method, http.route, http.response.status_code attributes are present
Span status is set to ERROR on 5xx responses
metrics: http.server.request.duration histogram is produced by auto-instrumentation
config: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME is configurable via environment variable
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT defaults to http://localhost:4318 or is env-driven
package.json dev script loads instrumentation file before app (--import or --require)
No hardcoded endpoints or tokens in source code
error_handling: Span status is set to ERROR on unhandled errors or 5xx responses
span.recordException is called on caught exceptions

evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to this Go key-value store service. The code is at examples/go/kvstore/
evals.expected_output: OTel SDK initialized in cmd/kvstore-server, otelhttp middleware wraps the ServeMux, OTLP exporter configured

sdk_init: OTel initialization is placed in a separate file or function, not inline in main()
TracerProvider is created with a Resource containing service.name
OTLPTraceExporter is configured
TracerProvider is registered globally via otel.SetTracerProvider()
TextMapPropagator is set via otel.SetTextMapPropagator()
Graceful shutdown is wired via defer in main() or signal handling
auto_instrumentation: otelhttp.NewHandler wraps the http.ServeMux returned by api.Handler()
spans: HTTP server spans are produced for routes: GET /kv/{key}, PUT /kv/{key}, DELETE /kv/{key}, GET /search?word=
Span names are low-cardinality (no key values embedded in span names)
http.request.method, http.route, http.response.status_code attributes are present
Span status is set to ERROR on 5xx responses (e.g. internal server error from persist failure)
metrics: http.server.request.duration histogram is produced by otelhttp middleware
http.server.active_requests gauge is produced by otelhttp middleware
config: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME is read from environment or set via resource attribute with value like kvstore
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT defaults to http://localhost:4318 or is env-driven
No hardcoded endpoints or tokens in source code
error_handling: Span status is set to ERROR on store errors that result in 5xx responses
span.RecordError is called on caught store errors
404 Not Found responses do not set span status to ERROR (expected client errors)
structure: OTel code does not break the existing multi-package layout (cmd/ vs kvstore/)
Existing store.go and http.go are minimally modified
SDK init lives in cmd/kvstore-server/ since that is the process entry point

evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to this partially-instrumented Go chi service. The code is at examples/go/chi-partial/
evals.expected_output: Existing anti-patterns fixed, missing MeterProvider added, hardcoded endpoint replaced with env var, error handling added

dependencies: go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/metric or otel/metric is added to go.mod if not already present
sdk_init: MeterProvider is configured and registered globally via otel.SetMeterProvider()
TextMapPropagator is set via otel.SetTextMapPropagator()
Hardcoded OTLP endpoint (collector.example.com) is replaced with env var or default localhost
Resource includes service.name from env var or explicit value
Graceful shutdown is wired via defer or signal handling
auto_instrumentation: otelhttp.NewHandler still wraps the chi router (existing, should be preserved)
spans: High-cardinality span name (GetTask-{id}) is fixed to a low-cardinality name
Tracer is moved from handler hot path to package-level variable
metrics: http.server.request.duration histogram is now produced (MeterProvider added)
error_handling: Span status is set to ERROR on failures
span.RecordError is called on caught errors

evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to this Spring Boot service. The code is at examples/java/springboot-basic/
evals.expected_output: OTel Java agent configured, OTLP exporter wired via env vars or system properties

dependencies: opentelemetry-javaagent.jar is downloaded or referenced in the project
No OTel SDK dependencies added to pom.xml (agent bundles its own SDK)
sdk_init: Java agent is attached via -javaagent JVM argument
JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS or application startup script includes the agent flag
No programmatic SDK initialization code is added (agent handles it)
auto_instrumentation: Spring MVC controllers are auto-instrumented by the Java agent (no code changes needed)
spans: HTTP server spans are produced for all routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/{id}, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/{id}, DELETE /tasks/{id}
Span names follow OTel HTTP semconv: low-cardinality route templates
Span status is set to ERROR on 5xx responses
metrics: http.server.request.duration histogram is produced by the Java agent
config: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME is configurable via environment variable or -Dotel.service.name
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT defaults to http://localhost:4318 or is env-driven
No hardcoded endpoints or tokens in source code
error_handling: Span status is set to ERROR on unhandled exceptions
span.recordException is called on unhandled exceptions by the agent
